![Observed recall rates and predictions from four selected pooled source-context models. Each model row shows Negative and Neutral recall rates using the red-and-black category convention of the empirical figures. The value at right is the Negative-minus-Neutral recall-rate difference. Observed error bars are percentile 95% confidence intervals from 10,000 participant-level bootstrap samples ($N=38$). Predicted error bars are central 95% intervals across 200 complete simulated datasets and represent simulation variability rather than parameter-estimation uncertainty. Simulations condition on each list's observed recall count.](recall_rate_diagnostic_figure.svg){#fig-model-recall-rates width="100%" fig-alt="Five model rows compare Negative and Neutral recall rates. Observed data show higher recall for Negative items. The model without categorical enhancement shows little difference, while all three categorical-enhancement models reproduce the observed Negative advantage."}
